question: is there a docker container which makes it possible to run the nomad python package on client side on windows? did someone thought about this? A user of mine deleted all of his uploads. I have backups including mongo dumps elastic etc. what is the best way to restore a selected set of uploads?
proposed_answer: Sorry for the late reply, I just saw this. I have no actual experience with this. Especially be careful with the `mongorestore` . Do dry runs and double check what i am writing here. **1. restore the mongo upload and entry documents.** Theoretically you should be able to use `mongorestore` (see our [docs](<https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html#backups>) and the mongo [docs](<https://www.mongodb.com/docs/database-tools/mongorestore/>)) on the whole backup as `mongorestore` supposidly on restores objects that are not already present (e.g. deleted). But I would really test this first; make sure you do not activate `--drop` (drops all collections before restoring them); use `--dryRun`. **2. put the files back** If you know the upload id, you could just put the right directories back. Otherwise, you have to look into rsync or something to put the right files back. You can also use the restored mongo uploads and try to use some mongoquery involving the user id or something. I guess, you are familier with the file layout. All uploads live in `.volumes/fs/[public|staging]/<first-letter-of-id>/<upload-id>` . You only need to put back those `<upload-id>` dirs that …

question: I guess then it is that way. I couldn't get netcdf4 to install when I did a pip install nomad. There is some odd error. I couldn't test the import. Do you know if we can choose [all] somehow from package.find?
proposed_answer: > Do you know if we can choose [all] somehow from package.find? I don't think that will work because the plugins are their own packages and not actually submodules of pynxtools. Essentially, they are their own packages that should be installed additionally. The best would be to install pynxtools[all] as dependency in nomad, as suggested by Florian in this MR: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/1629. Then, one could even install just one of the plugins in your local OASIS by overwriting in the pyproject.toml, e.g. pynxtools[mpes]. However, since this MR is currently breaking the tests and in general not working, the only workaround that I can think of is to add a manual install of pynxtools to the Dockerfile as we had it before: ``` WORKDIR /install/dependencies/parsers/nexus RUN pip install -r requirements.txt RUN pip install --no-deps ".[all]" WORKDIR /install ``` This is why I was asking if this manual installation is still done somewhere.

question: Is there any specific context in which you would like to make the CIF files searchable? The materials card in the overview page gets the data from `results.material` and `results.material.topology`. Basically, you could have a schema with a `structure_file` quantity that takes the CIF file(s) and populates this. We do something along this line in an experimental plugin that we are developing for XRD analysis. I think we should have support for a base section that provides this functionality out of the box. @Sarthak Kapoor, @Hampus Näsström, @Lauri Himanen, any thoughts?
proposed_answer: @ron_do Hi, pls feel free to link your project. It'll provide a lot of useful context to identify the issue. Most importantly is how you connected the CIF file to the schema. Here are the possibilities that I'd try out, in order: 1. It sounds like you're parsing the CIF file into `run.system`. In that case, this isn't really a calculation, hence why populating `results` fails. This is what @Joseph Rudzinski was pointing out. 2. If you want to upload structure files without any calculation attached, it'd be better to provide it as its own entry type. Then we can use `data`, which bypasses `results`, for visualization. 3. Or we don't use the native NOMAD visualizer and instead explore the NORTH tools.

question: Do we have already a standard NOMAD way for publishing optical spectroscopy data? How does this compare?
proposed_answer: Hey @Julia Schumann, very interesting. I will take a closer look next week. From the computational side, I thought a lot about this. Doing simpler data modeling and Python implementation, I came up with the concept of `SpectralProfile`. This is an abstract class which has defined the intensities as an array and some validations to check that these are defined positive numbers. This is then used to inherit in `DOS` and in `AbsorptionSpectrum`, which in turns is inherited in `XASSpectrum`. This is for data that we already have in the database. For UV-Vis, I'll argue that then it will be about doing the same than with XAS. And similarly for IR, Raman... Now, in terms of material properties, I think the question would be to define the AbsorptionCoefficient and possibly connected it with SpectralProfile, but this is not done yet and I guess people can have other ideas.

title: User ID for new oasis
question: Ok, so on my server there is a docker group of which I'm a user. ``` frijtersch@app-nomad01:~/nomad/tno-solar-nomad-image$ getent group | grep docker docker:x:986:frijtersch frijtersch@app-nomad01:~/nomad/tno-solar-nomad-image$ id uid=110867669(frijtersch) ``` In this case I would have to run`chown -R 110867669:986 .volumes` and in the docker-compose file set `users: 110867669:986` for the north config?
proposed_answer: No, `docker-compose.yaml`should read `users: 1000:986`. I can't say I understand it completely, but UID 1000 is hardcoded by nomad and needs to be the same inside the docker containers and the system. `sudo chown -R 1000 .volumes` should suffice. Note that there are security considerations with the docker group: https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/linux-postinstall/ > The docker group grants root-level privileges to the user.

question: I have rebased.. I skipped wherever required.. Could you please check if I have done it correctly?
proposed_answer: It somehow still has a different set of definitions compared to master. This is not intentional, right? Rebasing is tricky when you have submodules. If you are fine to use the definitions at master, can you run from the command line ``` git submodule sync --recursive git submodule update --init --recursive --remote --jobs=4 git submodule foreach --recursive 'git fetch --tags' cd src/pynxtools/definitions printf "$(git describe --dirty --tags --long --abbrev=8 --match '*[0-9]*')" > ../nexus-version.txt ``` That should set the definitions to the latest, which is also what we have in the master branch Also, you'll need to add owlready2, fastapi, and pygit2 to the pyproject.toml file under dependencies See this CI/CD workflow failing: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools/actions/runs/16651056418/job/47123603244?pr=680

question: I am trying to do something similar and agree with @Klaus Götz , the documentation should be improved. Do we have a minimal example somewhere? Do I need to set `self.creates_children = True` in the parser class that is inheriting from MatchingParser? And how do I need to structure the child_archives dict?
proposed_answer: yes, it should be set to the instance of `MatchingParser`, If is_mainifile returns a list of strings, then `child_archives` will be a key: archive dict. you can access this in your parse function, i.e. `def parse(self, mainfile, archive, logger, child_archives):` I have created an issue to add this in the docs here: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-docs/issues/17

title: general-comp
question: Hi everyone! This is perhaps a ridiculously simple question, but I am new to this area and to NOMAD. What is the simplest way to download all of the data from NOMAD (sync it locally) such that one can do simple things such as making a Venn-diagram of all of the different classes of materials that exist in NOMAD?
proposed_answer: Hi @chaxor ! Thanks for posting, any questions are welcome 😁 You can make an API call to query the NOMAD repository for certain information from certain entries or from all entries, without downloading the data (there are 112 TBs of data on NOMAD!). However, you may not even need to do this, depending on what you mean by "different classes of materials". If you go to the Calculations APP (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/gui/search/calculations) you will already find characterizations like "space group" and "crystal system" in the widgets on display by default. You can find further characterizations by clicking on the filter bars under `Materials` on the left hand side. NOMAD already provides many classifications of the material for you without you having to analyze the data yourself. If you share more specifically what kind of classification you are trying to achieve, we can direct you on the best route to get there 🤓

question: That sounds great! Nothing super urgent on my side but I definitely see some use cases where it could be handy 🙂 Do you want me to put this in a Gitlab issue or do you prefer to do it?
proposed_answer: I'm actually wrong about the first point: you can already do negation through the API. You can see an example in our API endpoint docs (`/entries/query`). You just need to wrap things in a dictionary with the `not` key. So what we could do is to introduce a search bar syntax for easily doing such queries. An easy step would be simply to have the `!=` operator, but in the long run we will probably want to negate some bigger logical groups (e.g. `!(C or H)`). So the overarching Gitlab issue should be about extending the search bar syntax. We just migrated the basic search functionality to the new GUI, and this would be a good point to extend the search bar functionality as well. I created an issue in the `nomad-gui` project: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-gui/-/issues/72

question: Hi everyone! How can we determine which plugin to use when reading a file and how do we navigate graphs on NOMAD?
proposed_answer: HI Astou, I'll try to provide some answers for the two questions: 1. What do you mean by "reading a file"? Do you mean when you upload a file to NOMAD? What parser gets called is decide by the matching criteria. The simplest criteria is to match on either the file name/ending or the mime type of the file using the `MatchingParser` class. You can find more information about this in the docs here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/explanation/processing.html#matching 2. Not sure exactly what you mean by graphs. NOMAD doesn't have a graph backend but we do have an option of finding the back references between entries. These are resolved in the GUI so to navigate in a graphical sense you can follow the references where they are place in the data section and the back references can all be found on the overview page. As far as I know we have no API endpoint for querying across multiple references, this would have to be done on the client side. Maybe if you provide some more information on what you want to do I could provide more information.

proposed_answer: Yeah in CI we use `uv pip` instead of standard pip for this. In `uv` the `@develop` will resolve to the actual commit SHA which is equal to what we have in requirements file so there's no conflict. But in `setup_dev_env.sh` pip is used, which is presumably trying to compare `@develop` with a sha and it doesn't match. I think we might have to update the dependency in workflow plugin here Or we can use `uv pip` in setup_dev_env.sh too

proposed_answer: Dear Markus, thank you very much! That already answered my question. Yesterday I forgot to add the context. I want to save data from photoluminescence spectroscopy experiments where we excite our samples with light in the visible and near-infrared spectral range and then collect the photoluminescence. For this we use a confocal microscopy setup. In the end we measure the collected photoluminescence using either a spectrograph or a single-photon detector such as a single-photon avalanche diode (SPAD) or a superconducting nanowire single-photon detector (SNSPD). As a first step I am currently implementing a Python function using h5py to save simple spectra from our spectrographs according to the NXoptical_spectroscopy specification. We wanted to have something with which we can already start saving our spectra according to a NeXus specification and then slowly build on this in the future. As far as I am aware of there is currently no [pynxtools extension](https://fairmat-nfdi.github.io/pynxtools/reference/plugins.html) available for this use case but maybe someone else here can point me in the right direction if there are already some efforts underway for this appdef. title: DOI generation
question: If we have our institutes datacite account should it in principle be possible to generate DOIs with nomad setting the correct configs?
proposed_answer: Looks that way in the code: ```python class Config(ConfigBaseModel): """Model for the NOMAD configuration.""" ⋮ datacite: DataCite = DataCite() ⋮ ``` ```python class DataCite(ConfigBaseModel): mds_host: str = 'https://mds.datacite.org' enabled: bool = False prefix: str = '10.17172' user: str = '*' password: str = '*' ``` So you only need to add the `datacite` entry to your `nomad.yaml`: ```yaml datacite: enabled: true ⋮ ``` I have NOT tried it myself.

title: Unexpected dependency version on NOMAD develop
question: I don't want a specific version though, I just always want the latest release version. I thought this would already happen with `"nomad-parser-plugins-atomistic>=1.0"` in the toml. The question is why is this stuck at the 1.0.2 release?
proposed_answer: In the `scripts/generate_python_dependencies.sh` it will only upgrade nomad-lab and nomad-lab-gui, no plugins are upgraded automatically so they will always keep their locked version. The intention here was that no other dependency gets automatically updated and individual plugin authors can upgrade their plugins as they see fit and we have a more controlled roll out. Otherwise, if random plugins get updated automatically during the nightly run, the pipeline might start failing due to changes in the plugin.

I only have the 3 infrastructure containers (elastic, rabbitmq, mongo) running and don't see anything special in their logs. The rest is running in the terminal. @Lauri Himanen How can I check if the worker is running properly?
proposed_answer: You can check the status of the services by running `docker ps`. This will show "STATUS" column that you can check. Also, you could inspect the live logs by removing the -d flag from the docker compose command: `docker compose up` instead of `docker compose up -d`. This will show you all of the logs from all of the services. You could then copy/paste this whole log here and we can inspect them for any signs of possible failure.

question: If I understand, the correct solution is that it inherit sdirectly from the base class, and this is what is returned, doesn't it?
proposed_answer: Yes, the actual problem we have is an NXcalibration in NXprocess_mpes. So the inheritance chain should be [`NXmpes:/ENTRY/PROCESS_MPES/CALIBRATION`, `NXprocess_mpes:CALIBRATION`, `NXcalibration`] but I just got [`NXmpes:/ENTRY/PROCESS_MPES/CALIBRATION`, `NXcalibration`] . With calling `get_inherited_nodes` with path and root node this gives [`NXmpes:/ENTRY/PROCESS_MPES/CALIBRATION`, `NXprocess_mpes`, `NXcalibration`], which is fine but I want to store the nodes directly instead of their xml roots Anyways, I replaced this with my own implementation which works now With this we probably could also untie this from dev_tools code. We only use `get_nx_namefit` and `get_nexus_version` in pynx. `get_nexus_version` is just a fallback and not really necessary the way we use this with the `nexus-version.txt`

question: Some link do not have any correct field or data path to be linked, Because the data is not provided by the raw file. Convertet can set a check, is the link to the data is available or not. If not found converter should remove the path that is linking to other data field. As this feature will be applied to all the readers, dataconverter can have this feature for all the readers. Does it make sense?
proposed_answer: This should be done by the reader When the dict ends up at validation this should already be resolved or deleted The first error: ``` WARNING - Field /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/current_amplifier/hardware written without documentation. WARNING - Field /ENTRY[entry]/INSTRUMENT[instrument]/hardware/@version written without documentation. ``` Is because `.../current_amplifier/hardware` is set as a field with a single value. In NXsts, however, it is a group of `NXfabrication` You probably need to add `/model` and `/model/@version` at the end

question: Can I tests this in the Oasis? Which scatter plot? Note that when you select a region in the scatter plot, you are essentially setting a filter like `500 K < temperature < 600 K AND 50 < conversion < 80`. If `temperature` is an array, this will include entries where temperature is e.g. [300, 400, 500]. But maybe first let me know how to reproduce this and then we can see if there is indeed a bug, or if the behaviour is simply unexpected and should be reconsidered.
proposed_answer: Yes, you can test this in the[Test Oasis](https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/search/heterogeneouscatalyst?results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Bgte%5D=510.1864404632021%20K&results.properties.catalytic.reaction.reaction_conditions.temperature%5Blte%5D=640.2867625380519%20K) it does show the first filter, e.g. for the temperature, but not the other axis, e.g. the feed composition, it also does not update the range that it shows in the widget. partly it is because an entry that falls within the search window also has data points outside. but there is some problem for the 2nd axis, probably because it is not specified further , e.g.reactants[*].conversion so it looks like it does not attempt any filtering along the 2nd axis

title: Issue Starting Nomad Server: Connection Problems with Services
question: @Lauri Himanen do you know what might be happening here?
proposed_answer: Hi @rosario.forlenza ! So I think that Elasticsearch is running fine, but could be that somehow WSL is not letting some of the connections through. I think you are following the `nomad-distro-dev` setup in which Elasticsearch is run through docker, and then the app+worker are run as a separate python process that is outside docker. Is this right? I'm not famiiliar with WSL and cannot really test this myself as I don't have a Windows machine available. Are you using Docker desktop wih the WSL2 backend? You could try the following: - If `docker compose` and `uv run poe start` are run in different WSL distributions, they cannot communicate by default. You could check this by running the command `wsl -l -v` in both terminals to verify that they run in the same WSL distribution. Also you could check if you can run `docker ps` in the terminal which you use to run `uv run poe start`.

question: Q: is it possible at the moment, from nexus entries to somehow link to other nomad entries, such as materials which are already documented in NOMAD?
proposed_answer: You could use the workflow in order to connect different entries with different upload_id and entry_id. You would do that by creating a new entry and defining the workflow in there (this is a `workflow.archive.yaml` file). This is totally agnostic of nexus, but you can add the path to the nxs mainfile in your workflow and hence keep the provenance of Nexus entry + material in Nomad.

title: general-expt
question: Thank you. just realized I already had the same question a few months ago :p although formulated differently. Got a different solution back then as I was proposed to just add the id of my older entry in the nexus file of my new entry. I assume the workflow offers me the additional possibility to connect entries after they are both already entered, right?
proposed_answer: Yes, you can even define a `workflow2` section inside the nxs entry. Workflow per se is just a way of organizing data, so you can decide how to do it at will. You can either decide to use the nxs entries as your basic building block and work with workflows **on top of it**, creating more entries using the `workflow.archive.yaml` I told you OR, you can put everything in the nxs entry by rewritting the nxs file with `entry_id`s. The decision here is just pure data model design, so you are free to go with whichever you prefer. Both options (breaking into multiple entries for workflows vs. all in one entry) have pros and cons. In Area A (synthesis) and C (computation), we go with the first option in a slightly different way.

title: discussion
question: Hi, I have a Section with a Quantity `process` of type `Reference(BaseProcess.m_def)` . In a `normalize `function I would now like to access the `entry_id` of the referenced file. Is there a method which can do this, like` self.process.m_... (’entry_id’)`?
proposed_answer: Hi Aaron: The combination of defining a `Package` and calling its `__init__metainfo__()` -method simply registers the definitions in that file into our system. If you leave these parts out, I think you can still regularly use the schema in your Python code, but the definitions will not be visible in our GUI code (=you can't e.g. instantiate entries from that schema). We hope that this package registration can be automated in a revision of the plugin system that we are working on.
